compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Why
Nothing currently checks the DIFF against its ticket. plan-review checks the plan; completion-verifier
checks at close; a diff-time scope check is complementary and catches scope drift in the change itself.

## What
New scope-intent overlay, content-triggered when >=1 ticket resolves from the commit's rebar-ticket:
trailer. Grounded prerequisites from plan review:
- REGISTER `scope-intent` in the closed OVERLAY_IDS tuple in registry.py (unknown ids are dropped).
- assemble_diff (assemble.py) does NOT currently read the rebar-ticket: trailer; add trailer parsing via
  extract_ticket_refs (supports multiple ids: multiple trailer lines AND whitespace-separated tokens) and
  assemble the referenced tickets' scope/AC as this overlay's context.
- Ticket-blindness is NOT free: ticket_context is threaded into the shared reviewer_input, so keeping base
  + other overlays ticket-blind requires explicit plumbing (gate ticket context to the scope-intent
  overlay only; do not leak it to the others). Verify with a test.
Checks the diff against the UNION of all referenced tickets so legitimate multi-ticket commits don't
produce false scope findings. Reuses plan-review's scope-intent criterion vocabulary. Advisory.

## Scope
reviewers/code-review-scope-intent.md, registry.py (OVERLAY_IDS + content trigger), assemble.py (trailer
read + ticket-context assembly), criteria_routing.json (SHARED — coordinate with C, F).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `scope-intent` in OVERLAY_IDS; overlay fires only when a ticket resolves from the trailer.
- [ ] assemble_diff reads the trailer (all ids) and assembles union scope context.
- [ ] A test proves base + other overlays receive NO ticket context (ticket-blind).
- [ ] A multi-ticket commit naming all its tickets produces no false scope-drift finding (eval).
- [ ] Overlay is advisory.

## Data-flow: commit message -> scope-intent overlay (resolves plan-review G6)
The trailer lives in the COMMIT MESSAGE, which is not part of the diff text, so it must be plumbed
end-to-end (default "" everywhere → overlay simply does not fire when absent, per the AC):
- `assemble.py`: `assemble_diff_context(...)` gains `commit_message: str = ""`. It calls
  `extract_ticket_refs(commit_message)`; for each resolved trailer ticket it loads that ticket's
  scope/AC and assembles a UNION scope-context string, exposed as a SEPARATE context field consumed
  ONLY by the scope-intent overlay (base + other overlays stay ticket-blind — assert this).
- `assemble_diff_input` schema: add optional `commit_message`.
- `gates/code-review.yaml`: thread `commit_message` into the `assemble_diff` step; the scope-intent
  overlay's `ticket_context` includes the union scope-context.
- `gate_dispatch.produce_code_review_verdict(...)`: accept + forward `commit_message` (default "").
- Call sites (`shim.review_code`, `review_bot/voter.py`): pass the change's commit message when
  available (Gerrit change commit body), default "" otherwise.
scope-intent is content-triggered (fires only when ≥1 trailer ticket resolves) and ADVISORY.

## Per-overlay context injection (resolves plan-review G6, batch-runner tension)
CodeReviewBatchRunner today injects one shared `_context` (the diff) into every overlay
(`batch_runner.py:58`). scope-intent needs a DIFFERENT ticket_context (the union ticket scope). Resolve
by extending the batch runner, not by polluting the shared context:
- `CodeReviewBatchRunner.__init__` gains `context_overrides: dict[str, str] | None = None` (prompt_id ->
  ticket_context). In `run`, when an overlay's `prompt_id` is in `context_overrides`, inject that string
  as its `ticket_context`; every other overlay keeps the shared diff `_context` (base + others stay
  ticket-blind — assert it in a test).
- `gate_dispatch.produce_code_review_verdict` builds `context_overrides={"code-review-scope-intent":
  <union_scope_context>}` ONLY when the commit-message trailers resolve ≥1 ticket (else omit → overlay
  inert), using the union-scope string assembled in assemble.py.
This is additive and default-None, so existing single-context behaviour is unchanged.

file_impact.reason: register scope-intent in OVERLAY_IDS + content trigger
read rebar-ticket trailer, assemble ticket context
scope-intent routing entry (shared-file: coordinate with C,F)

compiled_state.title: Scope-intent overlay (diff vs union of trailer tickets)
